mechanism_narrative: Sustained acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects broad-based economic strength and improving corporate operating conditions, leading to higher expected earnings and positive equity returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward Information Coefficient (IC) remains below 0.02 for a continuous 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a sharp deterioration in the labor market, a leading indicator of recession. This increases risk aversion and lowers growth expectations, causing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is accelerating, concurrent increases in the Fed Funds rate (FEDFUNDS) create a 'dual tightening' shock, aggressively compressing valuation multiples and raising recession risk, leading to sharply negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:2022 market performance during Fed tightening
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to produce a statistically significant negative return distribution (t-test p > 0.05) compared to the baseline during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime, strong retail sales (RSAFS) growth is interpreted negatively by markets, as it signals persistent demand-pull inflation that will force the central bank to maintain a hawkish stance, increasing the discount rate.
failure_condition: The correlation between RSAFS momentum and S&P 500 forward returns is positive and significant during a sustained period of high inflation (e.g., 2021-2023).

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals strong consumer balance sheets and confidence, boosting sentiment for cyclical sectors and leading to positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The hit rate (percentage of correct direction predictions) of the signal is less than 55% over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained, strong growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) is a fundamental driver of aggregate demand and corporate revenue, supporting higher equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The 12-month rolling correlation between PAYEMS 3-month growth and S&P 500 60-day forward returns is negative for more than 18 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A high absolute level of the Fed Funds rate makes cash and short-term bonds a more attractive alternative to equities (higher risk-free rate), leading to portfolio reallocations away from stocks and causing negative returns.
failure_condition: Average 60-day S&P 500 returns are not statistically lower when FEDFUNDS is above 4.0% compared to when it is below 4.0% over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Stronger than expected real GDP growth (GDPC1) revises aggregate earnings expectations upwards. This fundamental improvement in the economic outlook drives positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The beta of S&P 500 quarterly returns to quarterly real GDP growth surprises is not significantly positive (p > 0.10) over a full business cycle.
